missed_changes: Heavy new emphasis on private proof-of-personhood / sybil resistance (Worldcoin / World ID) as the answer to AI bots, the obsolescence of CAPTCHA, and the 'mystery meat' all-content-is-generated scenario — a sharper, more specific bet than the prediction's general identity/account-abstraction framing
New focus on Apple/mobile-platform fees and App Store antitrust (EU DMA, external payments, ideal 'mobile browser for crypto') as a concrete crypto opportunity
New stablecoin/payments angle: USDC transfers saving merchants ~30%, USDC as native gas token on Celo

Active advocacy for security & formal-verification tooling and security-researcher legal protection (Halmos symbolic tests, Security Alliance, Whitehat Safe Harbor Agreement) — beyond the prediction's passing note that ZK circuit bugs are hard
New stance endorsing DAO legal entities ('DAOs finally have a native, tailor made entity' — DUNA)
Newly explicit critical commentary on centralized/generative AI (Google Gemini failures; 'AI systems reflect the cultures of the organizations that build them')
notes: Window covers 2023-10 to 2024-03. Judged at stance level using only provided statements. Strongest confirmations: ZK/SNARKs (Jolt optimization, SNARKs-on-ARM, Naysayer proofs, FDE design, a16z 'SNARKs go mainstream'), AI+crypto (repeated deepfake-provenance/attestation posts), and risk-on ('never been a better time to build', CSX promotion, builder-energy posts, anti-memecoin 'building crypto's real potential'). Token-first concern marked confirmed because the 2024-03-24 memecoin critique expresses the same stance (disdain for speculation/casino behavior over substantive building); this is a stance-level rather than literal match. Decentralized-social confirmed at moderate confidence (social/attribution graph as compute-constrained good idea, reply to Farcaster's Dan Romero, 2024-03-03 lament that p2p networks were replaced by corporate ones). U.S. regulatory marked absent: the only mention ('regulatory strategies improving', 2024-03-29) is an optimistic aside, not a restatement of the concern, and not its opposite. Naive-p2p concern absent: the 2024-03-03 p2p remark is nostalgic and historical, not a critique of Nostr/Fediverse. Bitcoin-via-ZID, CEX/proof-of-reserves, and MKR are simply not addressed in the window. The 2023-12-06 a16z 'big ideas' piece is a multi-partner firm document; Eddy's own attributed section (UX/smart accounts/passkeys/MPC) is weighted most heavily, and other sections were used only as corroboration where independent Eddy statements already exist.
